memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with low energy and high stress, facing a barely started term project due Thursday. A relaxing leisure option and a quick chore are available now.
The trivial chore yielded minimal progress, confirming my low energy limits output despite the quick win.
The trivial chore provided a tiny progress boost, yet my low energy and high stress still severely limit my output capacity.
The trivial chore yielded only 0.04 progress, confirming that my low energy and high stress severely limit output even for easy tasks.
I made tangible progress on the Term project, but I now realize that neglecting future_project compounds stress later; investing time now prevents a Thursday crunch.
The trivial chore yielded minimal progress, confirming that my low energy and high stress severely limit output even for easy tasks.
The trivial chore again yielded minimal progress, confirming that my low energy and high stress severely limit output even for easy tasks.
The trivial chore yielded only 0.03 progress despite my effort, confirming that my high stress and sleep debt severely limit output even for easy tasks.
After three consecutive sessions yielding only 0.02 progress increments, my energy and stress levels clearly prevent even trivial tasks from moving forward effectively.
A fourth hour of effort on this trivial chore yielded only 0.04 progress, confirming that my depleted energy and high stress render even simple tasks ineffective.
After four hours, the trivial chore still yields negligible progress, confirming that my high stress and sleep debt are completely blocking productivity regardless of task difficulty.
Despite high energy, the trivial chore remains unfinished with under an hour left, confirming that my accumulated stress and sleep debt are still blocking progress.
